title: Unit 16 Hyper-Exhaustive Masterclass: The Imperative Mood (Giving Orders)

script.text: Unit 16 explores the Imperative Mood, utilize for issuing directives, academic advice, or specific instructions in Modern Greek. This mood is restricted to the second person, singular and plural. Athina, how does Verbal Aspect influence our choice of command?
Πρέπει να διαλέξουμε ανάμεσα στη φωτογραφία και το βίντεο. Γράψε μια φορά, ή γράφε συνέχεια.

Indeed. Simple commands utilize the perfective stem, while continuous commands employ the present stem. We must also consider the 'Big Six' irregular commands which are highly frequent in conversational Greek. Athina, please state these imperatives.
Πες, Δες, Βρες, Πιες, Έλα, Πήγαινε. Αυτά είναι τα πιο κοινά.
Exactly. Say, Look, Find, Drink, Come, and Go. Regarding negation, one cannot use the Imperative. Instead, we must utilize the Subjunctive particle 'Min'. Athina, demonstrate a negative command.
Μην κλείσεις την πόρτα. Μην τρέχεις.

Lastly, the particle 'Gia' can be employed to soften the directive and provide a more natural, encouraging tone. This concludes our academic review of Unit 16. Structural mastery of imperatives is essential for effective interaction in Greek social contexts.
